findings.title: Dependency on Real-Time Metering Pipeline
findings.description: The implementation of the usage-based billing engine (CM-301) is dependent on the development of a real-time metering pipeline that tracks usage accurately. This pipeline is critical for the rating engine and must be designed to handle high throughput.
assumptions: The existing infrastructure can support real-time data processing.
The team has experience with real-time data pipelines.

evidence.excerpt: Requires a metering pipeline that tracks usage in real time.

recommendations: Prioritize the design and implementation of the metering pipeline.

findings.title: Risk of Customer Churn During Pricing Migration
findings.description: There is a high risk of customer churn, especially among mid-market accounts, due to potential cost increases under the new pricing model. A proactive outreach strategy is necessary to mitigate this risk.
assumptions: Customers are sensitive to pricing changes.
The churn analysis model will accurately predict customer behavior.

evidence.excerpt: Flag accounts where projected spend increases >30% for proactive account management outreach.

recommendations: Develop a customer communication plan to address potential concerns.

findings.title: Implement Usage Alerts and Spending Caps
findings.description: To prevent bill shock, it is recommended to implement configurable spending alerts and optional hard spending caps as part of the MVP. This feature will enhance customer trust during the transition.
assumptions: Customers will utilize alerts and caps to manage their spending.
The system can handle the logic for alerts and caps.

evidence.excerpt: Customers need configurable spending alerts and optional hard spending caps.

recommendations: Include this feature in the MVP to enhance customer experience.

findings.title: Integration with Stripe for Invoicing
findings.description: The usage-based billing engine (CM-301) requires integration with Stripe for invoicing. This dependency must be addressed early in the development process to ensure timely billing.
assumptions: Stripe's API can accommodate the new billing model.
The team has experience with Stripe integration.
recommendations: Initiate the Stripe integration as soon as the billing engine design is finalized.

findings.title: Grandfather Existing Customers
findings.description: Implement a grandfathering system for existing customers to ensure a smooth transition to the new pricing model. This system should track customer cohorts and apply the correct pricing tiers.
assumptions: Existing customer data is accurate and accessible.
The system can handle cohort tracking.

evidence.excerpt: Need a grandfathering system that tracks customer cohorts.

recommendations: Prioritize this feature to maintain customer loyalty.

summary: The analysis highlights critical dependencies on the real-time metering pipeline and Stripe integration for the usage-based billing engine. There are significant risks related to customer churn during the pricing transition, necessitating proactive communication and features like spending alerts. A phased delivery plan is proposed with MVP focusing on essential billing functionalities and V1 addressing customer retention strategies.
